lines.en_lit: And what four or five; to the Rabbis, who say: incoming takes six and outgoing takes six, and no payment for closing the doors, from twelve must divide half less one, he takes five.
to Rabbi Yehuda, who said: incoming takes seven two are payment for closing the doors; outgoing takes five from ten he must divide Subtract one from half And takes four.
Rava said entire is Rabbi and he holds Rabbi Yehuda Rather, what four doesn’t he need to take five?
not difficult. This where there is a watch that is detained. That where there is not a watch that is detained,
If there is a watch that is detained, from eight must divide takes four. If there is not a watch that is detained, from ten he must divide takes five.
If so, what Rabbi says: always five difficult

MISHNA: provided with Elders from the Elders of the court, and they read before him the order of the day And they say to him: My Master, High Priest. Read with your own mouth, perhaps you forgot or perhaps you did not learn On Yom Kippur eve morning, stand him at eastern gate and pass before him bulls and rams and sheep so that he will be familiar and accustomed to the service, all the seven days they would not withhold from him food or drink on Yom Kippur eve at nightfall, they would not allow him to eat a great deal because food induces sleep
GEMARA: Granted, perhaps he forgot, fine, However, perhaps did not learn Do we appoint of that sort

But wasn’t it taught “And the priest who is greater than his brethren” that he be greater than his brethren in strength, in beauty, in wisdom, and in wealth. Aḥerim say: from where that if he does not have that his brethren the priests elevate him The verse states: “And the priest who is greater [haggadol] than his brethren”; elevate him [gaddelehu] from of his brethren.

Rav Yosef said: not difficult. There, the First Temple, Here, the Second Temple, As Rav Asi said: Marta, daughter of Baitos, brought a half-se’a of dinars in to King Yannai for that he appointed Yehoshua ben Gamla as High Priest.
On Yom Kippur eve morning, taught Even goats And the tanna our what is the reason he did not teach goats Since come for sins, become distraught.

If so, a bull as it too comes for sin. a bull, since for his and for his brethren the priests that it comes; among his brethren the priests, if there is a person who has a matter, would know and him back to through repentance. with regard to the entire Jewish people, he does not know
Ravina said this that people say: If son of your sister becomes a policeman [dayyala], see in the marketplace you do not pass before him.

all the seven days they would not withhold It was taught Rabbi Yehuda ben Nekosa says: they feed him fine flour and eggs in order to loosen his They said to all the more so that you bring him to a state of arousal.

It was taught Sumakhos said in the name of Rabbi Meir: One does not feed him Alef, beit, yod; and some say not Alef, beit, beit, yod; and some say neither white wine. Not alef, beit, yod neither etrog, nor eggs [beitzim], nor old wine [yayin]. And some say: Not alef, beit, beit, yod neither etrog, nor eggs [beitzim], nor fatty meat [basar], nor old wine [yayin]. And some say neither white wine because white wine brings man to impurity

the Sages taught: a zav, one attributes to food, or to all kinds of food, Elazar ben Pineḥas says in the name of Rabbi Yehuda ben Beteira: one feeds him neither Ḥet, gimmel, beit, yod, nor Gimmel, beit, mem, nor any items that bring him to impurity Not ḥet, gimmel, beit, yod neither milk [ḥalav], nor cheese [gevina], nor egg [beitza], nor wine [yayin]. And not gimmel, beit, mem neither soup of pounded beans [mei gerisin], nor fatty meat [basar], nor fish in brine [muryas].
Nor any items that bring him to impurity; what does it to include? It to include that which the Sages taught: Five items bring man to impurity And these are: Garlic,
